title: Best Practices and When to Use Context
description: Learn when Context is appropriate and how to organise it.

slides.title: When to Use Context? 🤔
slides.content:
# Context is Not for Everything

Context is great for **global data** that many components need:
- Theme (light/dark mode)
- User authentication status
- Language/locale
- Notifications

But avoid using Context for every piece of state – it can lead to unnecessary re‑renders and complexity. For component‑specific state, stick to local `useState`.

slides.title: Organising Context
slides.content:
## Best Practices

- **Separate contexts for different domains** – e.g., `AuthContext`, `ThemeContext`, `TodoContext`. Don't put everything in one giant context.
- **Place providers near the root**, but not necessarily at the very top if not needed everywhere.
- **Memoise context values** to prevent unnecessary re‑renders. Use `useMemo` when the value object changes often.
- **Create custom hooks** for each context to encapsulate usage and provide helpful errors.

slides.title: Custom Hook Example

quizData.question: When is Context most appropriate?

options.text: For all state to avoid prop drilling
For truly global data like theme or user
When you have less than three components

quizData.explanation: Context is best for data that many components need, not for every piece of local state.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: If you find yourself passing props through many levels, consider if Context is appropriate. If not, component composition (passing components as props) can also reduce prop drilling.
